overview: The Wausau City Council approved a significant $10+ million development agreement for 11 Scott Street (Wateride Place) by a 6-3 vote, which will convert a vacant 100,000 square foot building into 52 mid-priced residential units. The council also recognized city workers for their response to a record-breaking 30.9-inch snowfall and presented the 2026 Sustainability Award to Colby and Colby Millwork for their solar energy initiatives.

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Proclamation for Sarah Rafi and glioblastoma awareness
Mayoral citation for DPW snow response crews
Sustainability Award presentation to Colby and Colby Millwork
Development agreement for 11 Scott Street/Wateride Place
Solid waste and recycling service agreement with Harter's Fox Valley
Settlement resolution for David Holes vs City of Wausau

discussions.body: Mayor Denny read a proclamation honoring Sarah Rafi, a local attorney diagnosed with stage 4 glioblastoma brain cancer. Rafi is a Marquette Law graduate who has practiced law in Wausau for nearly 30 years and authored the book 'Be Happy in Both Worlds.' March 31st was proclaimed as Sarah Rafi Day in Wausau, coinciding with her birthday. The mayor noted that local Elk Lodge member Brad Gessel also recently passed from the same disease.

discussions.item: Mayoral citation for DPW snow response
discussions.body: Mayor Denny presented a mayoral citation to Department of Public Works plow crews and fleet staff for their response to a record-shattering 30.9-inch snowfall from March 14-16, 2026. Kevin Kester, street supervisor Josh, store room manager Mitch Harris, and fleet technician Jieven Matah were specifically recognized. Kester addressed the council, praising the plow operators and mechanics, noting that by Friday the technicians will have worked 12 straight days without a day off.

discussions.item: 2026 Sustainability Award to Colby and Colby Millwork
discussions.body: Sustainability committee members Christine Daniels and Gina Brew presented the 2026 City of Wausau Sustainability Award to Colby and Colby Millwork. Representatives Mike Thompson and Keith Kaning accepted the award and described their over 2,000 solar panel installation that became operational in July 2025, generating enough power for approximately 120 homes. They also highlighted LED lighting conversion and high-energy air compressor initiatives, plus comprehensive recycling programs for wood, aluminum, glass, vinyl, and other materials.

discussions.item: Development agreement for 11 Scott Street/Wateride Place
discussions.body: The council approved the development agreement with 11 Scott Street LLC by a 6-3 vote. Developers Raleigh Lray and Mark Craig spoke during public comment, explaining the $10+ million project to convert a vacant 100,000 square foot building into 52 mid-priced residential units. Alder Rasmusen supported the project, noting it returns parking spaces to public use and addresses the 'missing middle' housing need. Alder Neil emphasized the project generates $55,000 in annual parking revenue and helps TID 8 reach valuation goals for potential closure in 5 years. Alder Larson dissented, objecting to discounting city assets during budget cut times. Alder Tierney questioned the city's ability to provide alternative parking within 300 yards if the ramp closes, with Economic Development Director Randy Feifer explaining the existing agreement was reduced from 480 to 150 spaces.

discussions.item: Solid waste and recycling service agreement
discussions.body: The council approved a seven-year residential solid waste and recycling service agreement with Harter's Fox Valley Disposal unanimously 9-0. The mayor noted there had been a previous mix-up regarding whether the term was seven or ten years, which was corrected to align with the public health and safety committee's original recommendation.

discussions.item: Settlement of David Holes vs City of Wausau
discussions.body: Assistant City Attorney Vincent Bonito explained a settlement related to a 2022 bus accident where Transit Mutual Insurance paid the city's claim and the individual who hit the bus later filed a personal injury claim. The city's counter claim and third-party complaint against the individual's insurer resulted in the insurer agreeing to pay damages for the bus. Alder Neil clarified this settlement does not affect the individual's separate ongoing injury claim. Approved 8-1.

publicComment: Two speakers addressed the council. Raleigh Lray from 11 Scott Street LLC requested support for their Wateride Place project, describing it as a green sustainable repurposing of a vacant building into mid-priced apartments. Mark Craig of 3246 North 8th Street joined her, emphasizing the project exceeds $10 million, involves 100,000 square feet, and would not happen without city support, noting the previous term sheet vote was 7-4.
actionItems: Development agreement for 11 Scott Street/Wateride Place approved 6-3
Seven-year solid waste and recycling agreement with Harter's Fox Valley Disposal approved 9-0
Mayoral appointments to Plan Commission, Affordable Housing Task Force, and BID Board confirmed 9-0
Airspace obstruction removal agreements for 724/732 Ridgeland Avenue and 11 Ridgeland Avenue in Schofield approved 9-0
2026 budget modification for Police Department Red Dot Optics purchase using Thompson submachine gun sale proceeds approved 9-0
Solid waste disposal municipal code chapter 6.44 update approved 9-0 to align with state code
Paid duty time for out-of-country police training approved 9-0
Community outreach professional shelter operations duty premium differential approved 9-0
Settlement release for David Holes vs City of Wausau approved 8-1
March 31st proclaimed as Sarah Rafi Day in Wausau
Thomas Street Neighborhood Group meeting announced for Thursday with Police Chief Jeremy Cop discussing referendum
title: Wausau City Council Meeting - 3/24/2026 - 3/24/2026 - 3/24/2026
